mechanism_narrative: A sustained near-zero federal funds rate eliminates risk-free income alternatives, forcing institutional and retail capital into equities and compressing equity discount rates, which produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Median 20-day S&P 500 forward returns are non-positive during months when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 6.5% signals sufficient labor market slack to delay Federal Reserve policy normalization, extending the low discount rate environment and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 6.5%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered months over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in retail sales and industrial production indicate a coordinated demand-supply expansion that raises corporate revenue expectations and operating margins, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term of RSAFS and INDPRO fails to achieve a Rank IC above 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or the bivariate interaction adds no incremental predictive power over individual series (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of accommodative monetary policy, month-over-month acceleration in industrial production signals durable goods demand normalization that translates into higher cyclical corporate profits, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, positive month-over-month changes in INDPRO are followed by negative S&P 500 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An annualized inflation rate proxied by CPIAUCSL exceeding 2.0% triggers expectations of future monetary tightening that raise equity risk premiums and discount rates, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: low inflation transitioning to higher inflation
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change exceeds 2.0%, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances over any 2-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in payroll employment and declines in the unemployment rate confirm accelerating labor market dynamism that boosts aggregate wage income and consumption expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to produce positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of months, or the interaction term has zero incremental predictive power (t-stat < 1.5) in a bivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail and food services sales directly reflects consumer spending strength that drives approximately two-thirds of aggregate demand, lifting corporate revenues and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When RSAFS month-over-month growth exceeds 0.5%, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered observations over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Residential construction activity proxied by housing starts above 950 thousand units signals durable housing market strength that drives cyclical employment and materials demand, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST exceeds 950, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered months over any 3-year rolling window.
